chapter_title.ar: باب مِقْدَارِ الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُجْزِئُ لِلْوُضُوءِ وَ الْغُسْلِ وَ مَنْ تَعَدَّى فِي الْوُضُوءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – Measurement of the water which suffices for the Ablution (Wudhu) and the washing, the one who exceeds in the Ablution (Wudhu)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح أو حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/67)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح أو حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/67)
text: الْجُنُبُ مَا جَرَى عَلَيْهِ الْمَاءُ مِنْ جَسَدِهِ قَلِيلُهُ وَ كَثِيرُهُ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel, from Zurara,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one with sexual impurity, whatever water flows upon his body, be it little or a lot, so it has sufficed him’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Jamil from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘In taking a Ghusl (bath) because of sexual relations it is sufficient to allow water flow, a small or plenty of water over one’s body. This is sufficient for such Ghusl (bath).”’
